task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-12. Added site/components/site/MarketingShell.tsx (main + Nav + children + Footer). All former Nav/main/Footer pages and DetailShell use it; pnpm exec tsc --noEmit clean.

task.goal: Medium: add SiteSectionIntro (or equivalent) compound component wrapping SiteSectionHeading hero-compact + SiteProse mt-5; migrate landing *Intro.tsx files to shrink line count.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-12. SiteSectionIntro in components/site; landing intros + LineageCopy + InsightsPageIntro + PlayableTeaser use it; optional titleClassName/proseClassName for Lineage.

task.goal: Medium: extract shared Playable pieces (run chip row, embed + side panel layout, modal wiring) between Playable and PlayableTeaser.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-12. PlayableEmbedBlock.tsx shares PlayableEmbed + PlayableSelectedPanel grid; PlayableDocModal stays at section level (same wiring). Teaser vs full use different run selectors (chips vs PlayableRunGroups) by design.

task.goal: Write `references/proto-skills.md` as the canonical definition of proto-skill as a permanent skill type: pipeline stages, filesystem handoff contract (CANDIDATE.md + optional CONTEXT.md + REFERENCES.md), batching, promotion with PROVENANCE.md, and relationship to gad-skill-creator. Linked from the Reference nav and cited by decisions gad-167 and gad-168.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. `references/proto-skills.md` written covering pipeline stages, input/output contracts, batching rationale, context hydration pattern (calls gad snapshot/decisions/phases itself), validator semantics, and the create-proto-skill vs gad-skill-creator split.

task.goal: Fix /findings/<slug> 404: ship `site/app/findings/[slug]/page.tsx` using generateStaticParams over FINDINGS catalog so the links from LineageMeasurementSection, RunHeroCallouts, and rubric hero resolve to standalone whitepaper-style pages.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. [slug]/page.tsx renders title/summary/badges/bodyHtml with a back link to /findings. generateStaticParams emits one entry per finding in the catalog. generateMetadata returns per-finding titles.

task.goal: Rename `skills/gad-quick-skill/` → `skills/create-proto-skill/`. Update SKILL.md title/description to describe the filesystem handoff input contract and batching workflow. Update callers: `skills/gad-evolution-evolve/SKILL.md`, AGENTS.md, any site copy mentioning gad-quick-skill. Neutral upstream baseline (dot-agent create-skill) stays untouched — decision gad-168.

Validator v2 fix: patch `lib/evolution-validator.cjs` extractFileRefs to dedup overlapping path prefixes so a long-form path and its trailing substring are treated as the same reference. ~10 line change, add a unit test covering the dedup case.

Whitepaper-framed /findings index copy: update the intro paragraph and visual treatment on the /findings list page to read as a whitepaper library rather than an internal scratchpad. Summary badges, projects filter chips, sort-by-date control. Decision gad-169.

Implement create-proto-skill using the bulk + per-candidate-checkpoint pattern locked by decision gad-171. At startup: enumerate `skills/candidates/`, filter to candidates without a matching `.planning/proto-skills//{PROVENANCE.md,SKILL.md}` pair, draft each pending candidate in turn. For each: write PROVENANCE.md FIRST as a lock marker (candidate slug, phase id, pressure metrics, timestamp), then SKILL.md in dot-agent format. On crash/resume the skill picks up exactly where it left off because half-written proto-skills are identifiable by the PROVENANCE+no-SKILL state. Also update `gad evolution status` to render pending / in-progress / complete counts so partial runs are visible at a glance.
Design the workflow planning-artifact schema and write `.planning/workflows/README.md`.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. README defines kebab-case slug-per-file layout, YAML frontmatter (slug/name/description/trigger/participants[skills,agents,cli,artifacts]/parent-workflow?/related-phases?), exactly one mermaid fenced block per file, 8-12 node guideline, verbs-for-node-names rule, nesting via parent-workflow with mermaid subgraph rendering, workflow_conformance formula ((matching − extra − out_of_order) / expected), workflow_enter/workflow_exit trace event contract. Captures decisions gad-172 + gad-173.

task.goal: Author the first set of hand-written workflows: gad-loop, gad-decide, gad-evolution, gad-debug, gad-discuss-plan-execute, gad-findings.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. All six workflows authored under `.planning/workflows/`: gad-loop (top-level), gad-discuss-plan-execute (parent of gad-decide and gad-debug), gad-decide (pros/cons→recommend→commit), gad-debug (scientific-method with persisted .planning/debug/.md), gad-evolution (candidate→proto-skill→promoted/discarded), gad-findings (experiment→whitepaper→publish). Nesting tree: gad-loop → {gad-discuss-plan-execute → {gad-decide, gad-debug}, gad-evolution, gad-findings}.

task.goal: Extend `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs` to discover workflow files, parse frontmatter + mermaid body, and emit a WORKFLOWS array into `catalog.generated.ts` with a typed interface.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Added WORKFLOWS_DIR constant, parseWorkflows() helper (reads `.planning/workflows/*.md`, excludes README.md, parses frontmatter via existing parseFrontmatter, extracts mermaid fenced block via regex, warns + skips files with no mermaid block), Workflow + WorkflowParticipants typed interfaces (slug, name, description, trigger, participants[skills/agents/cli/artifacts], parentWorkflow, relatedPhases, origin, mermaidBody, bodyHtml, file), WORKFLOWS export in catalog.generated.ts. Scan output now reports `workflows=N` alongside skills/agents/templates. Verified: 6 workflows parsed cleanly on first run, pnpm exec tsc --noEmit clean.

task.goal: Trace-to-workflow synthesis v1: compute actual graphs for authored workflows + emit conformance scores.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs` gains `synthesizeWorkflowTraceData()` that reads `.planning/.trace-events.jsonl` (1000 events), builds participant matchers per authored workflow (CLI regex, artifact substring, skill trigger_skill), matches tool_use/file_mutation events to participants, collapses consecutive duplicates, emits React-Flow-shaped {nodes, edges} as `workflow.liveGraph`, computes `workflow.conformance = {score, matched, extra, out_of_order, expected}` per decision gad-173. Score formula: `max(0, matched − extra) / expected`. v1 returns 0/6 matches on the current escape-the-dungeon-heavy trace (expected — trace is eval data, not GAD framework work) which is enough signal to prove the pipeline is live. New Workflow interface fields: `liveGraph`, `conformance`, `support`. WorkflowCard renders conformance + support badges in header and passes liveGraph to WorkflowLiveDiagram. tsc clean.

task.goal: Workflows tab on /planning with authored (Mermaid) + live (React Flow) + emergent sections. Decisions gad-172 + gad-174.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Three-section layout live end-to-end: (1) Authored — 6 hand-authored workflows rendering Mermaid on the left and React Flow on the right, with conformance badge in each card header (advisory score from 42.3-04). (2) Emergent — 12 detector candidates rendering alongside authored via the same WorkflowCard component with support-count badges and "emergent" origin pill. (3) Noise panel — deferred to 42.3-13, placeholder section header with "deferred" note. Files: PlanningWorkflowsTab.tsx, WorkflowCard.tsx (now reads liveGraph + conformance + support), WorkflowMermaidDiagram.tsx, WorkflowLiveDiagram.tsx (React Flow v12 with stable NODE_TYPES), workflow-tree.ts. Tab wired into PlanningTabbedContent with count=18. @xyflow/react v12.10.2 installed. tsc clean for all new files.

task.goal: Resolve the three phase-42.3 open design questions: (1) workflows nest, (2) workflows carry validators, (3) agents self-report workflow identity.
task.resolution: Resolved 2026-04-14 in decision gad-173. (1) Yes — via `parent-workflow` frontmatter, mermaid subgraph rendering. (2) Yes — the validator IS the expected/actual graph diff, emits `workflow_conformance = (matching − extra − out_of_order) / expected`, upgrades flat `framework_compliance` (gad-162). No separate validator schema. (3) Yes — explicit `workflow_enter`/`workflow_exit` trace events are authoritative when present; sequence inference is the fallback for legacy/non-GAD-aware skills.

task.goal: Author the remaining four workflows started in 42.3-02: gad-evolution, gad-debug, gad-discuss-plan-execute (parent of gad-decide), gad-findings.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 alongside 42.3-02. All four files follow the schema in `.planning/workflows/README.md`: frontmatter with slug/name/description/trigger/participants/parent-workflow/related-phases + exactly one mermaid fenced block with 8-12 node flowchart. gad-discuss-plan-execute nests under gad-loop and parents gad-decide + gad-debug. gad-evolution and gad-findings nest under gad-loop directly.

task.goal: Rename the self-eval headline metric from `framework_compliance` to `workflow_conformance`.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `site/scripts/compute-self-eval.mjs` now emits `metrics.workflow_conformance` as the primary field, with `metrics.framework_compliance` retained as a deprecated alias for one version so existing dashboards don't break. Console summary string updated from "framework compliance" to "workflow conformance". Decision gad-173. Live verified: `[self-eval] 4607 events, 261 tasks (217 done), 171 decisions ... (overhead: 24.0%, workflow conformance: 5%, hydration: 77.4%)`.

task.goal: Emergent workflow detector v1 per decision gad-174.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as `detectEmergentWorkflows()` in `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs`. v1 approach: builds a sequence of tool names from tool_use events, slides an n-gram window (length 3..5), counts occurrences, filters to support ≥3, suppresses tuples dominated by longer tuples with equal support, and drops candidates whose CLI signatures match any authored workflow. Emits top `max_emergent` (12) candidates ranked by `length × support`. Each candidate becomes a full Workflow entry with `origin: "emergent"`, `support: { phases, stability }`, React-Flow-shaped liveGraph, and an auto-generated mermaid body so it renders in the same components as authored workflows. Thresholds centralized in WORKFLOW_MINE_THRESHOLDS constant (will move to .planning/config.json in a hardening pass). Live verified: 12 candidates detected from 1000 trace events — top patterns are bash→bash→bash (206-271×) and bash→read→edit (29×), matching observed eval-agent tool-use rhythm.

task.goal: Proto-workflow filesystem layout mirroring proto-skill (decision gad-174).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as `.planning/workflows/emergent/README.md`. Documents: lifecycle (recurring pattern → emergent candidate → proto-workflow → promoted authored), frontmatter schema (origin: emergent, support: {phases, stability}, evidence: [trace_instance_ids]), generation policy (v1 regenerates every build in-memory, persists only on promote so no churn), promotion flow (gad workflow promote writes `.planning/workflows/.md` with inlined provenance), discard semantics (re-detected if still above threshold; raise thresholds to suppress), and the four thresholds table with defaults (min_support=3, min_length=3, max_length=5, max_emergent=12).

task.goal: `gad workflow status | validate | promote | discard` CLI subcommands mirroring `gad evolution`.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as `workflowCmd` in `bin/gad.cjs`. Four subcommands: `status` lists authored (from catalog with conformance scores + parent chain) and emergent candidates (with support counts) and suggests next actions; `validate` shells out to `build-site-data.mjs` to refresh the catalog against current trace data; `promote [--name X]` reads the emergent entry from catalog.generated.ts and writes `.planning/workflows/.md` with frontmatter (slug, name, description, trigger, participants, parent-workflow: null, related-phases, origin: authored, provenance block with emergent-slug + support + promoted-at) + inlined mermaid body from detector output; `discard ` acknowledges and explains how to suppress permanently via threshold tuning. Live verified: `gad workflow status` from vendor/get-anything-done/ prints 6 authored + 12 emergent with support badges.

task.goal: Emergent section on the /planning Workflows tab (decision gad-174).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 alongside 42.3-05. Emergent workflows enter the WORKFLOWS catalog via `detectEmergentWorkflows()` with `origin: "emergent"` and render through the same WorkflowCard component that renders authored workflows, surfaced under the "Emergent workflows" section header in PlanningWorkflowsTab.tsx. Support count rendered as a badge in the card header. Each card shows the detector-generated mermaid diagram on the left and its React-Flow graph on the right. Inline promote/discard buttons deferred to a follow-up task — current CLI surface (`gad workflow promote/discard`) is sufficient for v1.

task.goal: Noise panel on the /planning Workflows tab — separate surface from the signal sections per decision gad-174. Two views: (a) raw event timeline (filterable by phase, session, event type) for events the emergent detector could not absorb into any candidate, (b) unmatched force-directed graph of the same events. Dense clusters in the noise graph are candidates for "lower the thresholds"; sparse clusters are genuine noise. Explicitly deferred — ship after 42.3-09 through 42.3-12 prove the signal surfaces are useful.
Hook-level trace event scope tagging (decision gad-175 v2).
task.resolution:
Shipped 2026-04-14. `bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs` now stamps every emitted event with `scope: "gad-framework" | "eval-agent" | "brood-project"` via a new `classifyEventScope()` helper that reads payload.cwd and environment variables at emission time. Rules (in order): `GAD_EVAL_TRACE_DIR` set → eval-agent; `GAD_BROOD_PROJECT_ID` set → brood-project; cwd contains `.claude/worktrees/agent-*` or `/worktrees/agent-*` → eval-agent; cwd contains `/evals/<project>/game/` → eval-agent; otherwise gad-framework. build-site-data.mjs `classifyTraceEventScope()` now prefers the explicit scope field on the event and falls back to the path-based classifier only for legacy events written before the hook update. Smoke-tested with both gad-framework and eval-agent synthetic payloads — classifier returns correct scope, event JSONL contains `scope` field.
Explicit `workflow_enter`/`workflow_exit` events are still queued as a separate deliverable — they become part of detector v2 (42.3-16) rather than v1 scope tagging. Design doc captures why: the task-registry-plus-trigger_skill merge in v2 gives us enough signal without requiring new event types.

task.goal: Workflow detector v2 scaffolding: skill-level mining with tool-level fallback (decision gad-178).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Three pieces wired end-to-end: (1) `bin/gad.cjs` gains a top-level `--skill <slug>` flag intercepted before citty parses — sets `GAD_ACTIVE_SKILL` env var and strips the flag from argv. (2) `bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs` `readActiveSkill()` now prefers `process.env.GAD_ACTIVE_SKILL` over the legacy marker file, so any tool_use event emitted inside a `gad --skill X` invocation gets stamped with `trigger_skill: X` automatically. (3) `detectEmergentWorkflows()` in build-site-data.mjs now checks whether any events carry `trigger_skill`; if `taggedEvents.length >= min_length` it mines the skill-tagged stream (v2), otherwise falls back to raw tool names (v1). Each emitted candidate carries a `detector: "skill-level (v2)" | "tool-level (v1)"` field that WorkflowCard renders as a color-coded badge (sky for v2, amber for v1) so readers can tell them apart. Live verified: "emergent source: tool-level (v1 fallback) — no trigger_skill tags yet, mining raw tool names" — the correct state until skills start migrating to `gad --skill` calls. The skill migration itself is a separate gradual task, not blocking for this detector scaffolding. Design locked in `.planning/notes/workflow-detector-v2-design-2026-04-14.md`.

task.goal: Publish /workflows/methodology (or equivalent) on the site documenting how the workflow detector actually works: trace-event schema, scope classification, participant matching for authored workflows, conformance formula (decision gad-173), v1 tool-level emergent mining vs v2 skill-level target (decision gad-178), and the three-section display (authored / emergent / noise). Include small pipeline diagram. This page is load-bearing for making the /planning Workflows tab legible to readers other than the operator — right now the methodology lives only in DECISIONS.xml and .planning/workflows/README.md, neither of which are on the site. Ship after the 42.3-16 deep-dive so the doc reflects the real v2 model, not the v1 shortcut.
Emergent workflows render React-Flow-only (no synthetic Mermaid), in a compact grid layout on /planning, with skill-named authored Mermaid diagrams (decisions gad-175, gad-177).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as a revision pass over phases 42.3-04/05/09/12. Changes: (1) `detectEmergentWorkflows()` emits empty `mermaidBody` and `undefined` conformance for emergent entries — nothing to diff against. (2) `WorkflowCard` branches on origin: authored = two-pane grid + metadata header + participant badges; emergent = single-column compact card, React Flow only, emerald border, minimal header. (3) New `compact` prop on `WorkflowCard` and `WorkflowLiveDiagram` (h-56 vs h-[480], no MiniMap, truncated description). (4) `PlanningWorkflowsTab` renders the emergent section as `md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3` so 12 candidates fit in 4 rows instead of 12. (5) `loadTraceEvents({ scope: "gad-framework" })` filters eval-agent events via path heuristic — 31/1000 filtered, 969 kept. (6) `parseFrontmatter` rewritten to handle one level of nested YAML so `participants:` sub-keys work; fixed the bug where 0/6 authored workflows matched anything. Live: 4/6 authored workflows now have live graphs. (7) All six authored workflow .md files updated to name skills explicitly in mermaid nodes (check-todos, task-checkpoint, gad-discuss-phase, gad-plan-phase, gad-execute-phase, gad-verify-work, debug, gad-debug, eval-report, write-feature-doc, create-proto-skill, gad-evolution-evolve, gad-evolution-validator, gad-skill-creator, gad-list-phase-assumptions). Skill names also added to participants frontmatter.

task.goal: Scaffold ContextFramework as a new top-level catalog type parallel to skills/agents/workflows/tech-stacks (decision gad-179).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. New TypeScript interface `ContextFramework` in catalog.generated.ts (slug, name, description, version, extends?, skills[], agents[], workflows[], canonicalProjects[], bodyHtml, bodyRaw, file). New `.planning/context-frameworks/` directory with three source files: `bare.md` (control condition, empty bundle, decision gad-164 baseline), `gsd.md` (upstream living comparison target, decision gad-165), `gad.md` (current framework with full skill/agent/workflow bundle referencing existing catalog items by slug). New `parseContextFrameworks()` helper in build-site-data.mjs mirrors `parseWorkflows()` — reads frontmatter, supports `extends` for framework inheritance, emits `CONTEXT_FRAMEWORKS` export. Live verified: `[context-frameworks] parsed 3 framework(s)`. Does NOT ship the /context-frameworks index page or detail pages — those are 42.4-02.

task.goal: /context-frameworks index page + /context-frameworks/[slug] detail pages.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `site/app/context-frameworks/page.tsx` lists all registered frameworks as a 3-column grid of card links — version badge, extends badge, description, skills/agents/workflows count triad, canonical project chip. `site/app/context-frameworks/[slug]/page.tsx` detail page resolves each referenced slug against the live SKILLS/AGENTS/WORKFLOWS catalogs and renders `BundleSection`s per type — unresolved references get an amber "unresolved" badge so stale bundles surface visually. generateStaticParams over CONTEXT_FRAMEWORKS for SSG, generateMetadata per framework. Detail page includes a breadcrumb back to the index, the framework's extends chain as a link, the `file` path, canonical projects as /projects/[slug] links, and the framework body rendered as HTML. Also added `/context-frameworks` to the Catalog nav group so it sits alongside Skills and Agents per decision gad-179. tsc clean.

task.goal: Project metadata gains a `contextFramework` field resolved from gad.json.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `parseEvalProjects()` in build-site-data.mjs reads `context_framework` first, falls back to the legacy `workflow` field so existing gad.json files don't need a mass migration. Value `emergent` folds into `custom` per decision gad-164 since it's no longer a first-class matrix condition (gad-166). `EvalProjectMeta` interface gains `contextFramework: string | null` and documents the legacy `workflow` field as @deprecated. Downstream surfaces (project market cards, lineage, comparison views) can consume `contextFramework` going forward. No gad.json files touched — migration is opt-in. Coordinated with phase 43's species schema unification so the canonical name is ready when 43 lands.

task.goal: Patch `gad snapshot` lane bootstrap so plain project snapshots do not auto-register idle agent lanes, and prune stale unclaimed default lanes that were leaked by the old behavior. Capture the skill-catalog consolidation rule for duplicate public skill names.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Plain `gad snapshot --projectid ` no longer auto-registers a lane just because a runtime is detected; bootstrap now requires explicit assignment intent (task scope, phase scope, parent/agent id, or explicit role context). Patched both live CLI path (`bin/gad.cjs`) and query-layer sources (`sdk/src/query/scoped-snapshot.ts`). Also updated lane listing cleanup in `lib/agent-lanes.cjs` and `sdk/src/query/agent-lanes.ts` so stale lanes with no claimed tasks or phases are marked released automatically instead of lingering forever. Verified locally: repeated project snapshots keep `agent: null` and do not mint `claude-default-0012+`. Logged follow-on decision gad-180: one canonical skill body per public skill name; duplicate same-name skill bodies are a catalog bug and must be merged, rewritten, or archived out of the canonical `skills/` tree.

task.goal: Normalize the command-skill catalog so procedural legacy duplicates are consolidated into `gad-*` workflows/references, keep only canonical `gad-*` command skills in `skills/`, and make the naming contract explicit.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Consolidated the duplicate plain-name command skills by moving their salvageable long-form methodology into prefixed workflows and leaving the `gad-*` command skills as the only canonical runtime-facing entries. Renamed authored workflow files to `workflows/gad-plan-phase.md`, `workflows/gad-new-project.md`, `workflows/gad-map-codebase.md`, and `workflows/gad-execute-phase.md`; added new prefixed workflows for `gad-debug` and `gad-eval-run`; updated canonical `gad-*` skills/commands/tests to reference the new workflow names; and removed the duplicate plain-name skill directories (`skills/plan-phase`, `skills/new-project`, `skills/map-codebase`, `skills/debug`, `skills/eval-run`, `skills/execute-phase`) from the canonical catalog. The procedural/methodology content now lives where it belongs: workflows for ordered execution and references for policy/background. Verified with targeted searches and site-data regeneration that live source paths now resolve through the prefixed `gad-*` workflow contract and the duplicate plain-name skills are gone from the shipped surface.

task.goal: Finish the remaining canonical-skill cleanup outside the first six command pairs and add an automated validator so duplicate public names and broken canonical references fail fast.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Removed the remaining duplicate plain-name skill directories that still exposed the same public `gad:` identities as their canonical `gad-*` counterparts (`add-todo`, `auto-conventions`, `check-todos`, `eval-bootstrap`, `eval-report`, `eval-suite`, `manuscript`, `reverse-engineer`). Renamed the remaining plain-name canonical skill directories that still exposed `gad:` identities to `gad-*` filesystem names (`milestone`, `session`, `snapshot-optimize`, `task-checkpoint`, `verify-phase`, `write-feature-doc`, `write-intent`, `write-tech-doc`). Added `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` plus the `check:assets` package script to enforce two invariants: no duplicate public skill names in canonical `skills/`, and no broken `@workflows/@references/@templates/@agents/@hooks` references across canonical framework assets. Live verified: `node scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` now passes after the cleanup.

task.goal: Move staged proto-skills into the owning project's `.planning/` tree, add a direct proto-skill install command for coding-agent runtimes, and align evolution docs/state so staged proto-skills are treated as planning artifacts until explicit install or promotion.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Moved the canonical proto-skill staging path from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/` in the live evolution CLI and supporting docs. `gad evolution validate|promote|discard|status` now read staged proto-skills from `.planning/proto-skills/`, promotion targets canonical `skills/` instead of the stale `sdk/skills/` path, and promotion preserves the full staged bundle (including provenance) instead of dropping metadata. Added `gad evolution install <slug>` with local/global runtime flags (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`) so a staged proto-skill can be installed into coding-agent skill directories for testing without promoting it. Updated proto-skill references, workflow docs, self-eval candidate generation, and planning notes to treat proto-skills as project-local planning artifacts first and framework DNA only after explicit promotion.

task.goal: `gad projects init` defaults to canonical XML and scaffolds the full file set. Currently it writes 4 markdown template files (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md) containing literal template placeholders. The rest of the monorepo uses 6+ canonical XML files (STATE.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml, REQUIREMENTS.xml). Surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding — the markdown output had to be hand-converted to XML, see ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. Fix: update `bin/gad.cjs projects init` (or wherever the scaffold lives) to write the canonical XML file set with valid empty headers. Optional `--format md` flag for the legacy markdown path. Also scaffold the missing files (TASK-REGISTRY, DECISIONS, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS) regardless of format.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Rewrote `projectsInit` in `bin/gad.cjs` against the contract in `references/project-shape.md` §5 (decision gad-185). Default format is now `xml` and scaffolds six files: the canonical minimum (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) plus the two strongly-recommended optionals called out in the task goal (`REQUIREMENTS.xml`, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`). Each template is a literal string from project-shape.md §5 with project id and today's date substituted — no `{{placeholder}}` tokens leak. `STATE.xml` seeds `status=active`, empty `current-phase`, and `next-action="Project initialized. Define requirements."`; `ROADMAP.xml` seeds a single `phase id="00" status="planned"`; `TASK-REGISTRY`/`DECISIONS`/`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS` are empty roots; `REQUIREMENTS.xml` includes a TODO comment. Added `--format md` flag that restores the legacy markdown scaffold but still writes `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` / `DECISIONS.xml` / `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` so the resulting root is canonical-minimum-clean regardless of format. Added `--projectid` arg (falls back to slug of `--name`). Added a pre-flight overwrite check: if any target file already exists, refuse with the blocker list unless `--force` is passed. Preserved the existing `gad-config.toml` root-registration behavior. Added a "Next steps" hint pointing at `gad projects audit`, `gad discuss-phase`, and `gad plan-phase`. Verification: `gad projects init --projectid test-xml --name "Test XML"` in a temp dir produced the six XML files, all parse under `fast-xml-parser`, canonical_minimum check is 4/4; re-run without `--force` was refused with the six-file blocker list; re-run with `--force` overwrote cleanly; `--format md` in a fresh temp dir produced the four legacy markdown files plus the three XML ledgers. Incident that prompted this task: ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. No new decision — `gad-185` frames the canonical shape and remains the authoritative reference for the init contract.

commands.command: node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs projects init --projectid test-xml --name "Test XML"
node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs projects init --projectid test-md --name "Test MD" --format md

task.goal: Project shape format review and refresh. The "canonical XML file set" for a planning root has drifted over time (some projects have HUMAN-TODOS.xml, some have phases/, some have plans/, some have proto-skills/, some have notes/). There is no single documented shape that `gad projects init` and `gad health` agree on. Audit the actual file sets across all registered planning roots (global, get-anything-done, magicborn, app-forge, etc.), define the canonical minimum + canonical optional file set, document it in references/, and align both `projects init` and `gad health` against it. Surfaced 2026-04-14 by the user during app-forge scaffolding: "the project shape format needs to be reviewed/updated soon."
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Audited all seven registered planning roots (`get-anything-done`, `global`, `app-forge`, `magicborn`, `grime-time`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`) by listing every `.planning/` directory and building a present/absent matrix across every file and directory observed. All 7/7 roots are canonical-minimum-clean on the new four-file bar (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) — no backfill needed. Wrote `vendor/get-anything-done/references/project-shape.md` (normative, 7 sections) defining: purpose + forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14 citation, canonical minimum (4 files with explicit note that `PROJECT.xml` is NOT promoted because it is present in 0/7 roots), canonical optional (9 files + 17 directories with per-entry conditions), legacy/deprecated list (8 file names + 1 directory name with deprecation reasons and replacements), per-file minimum valid XML headers for all six scaffold files (what 42.4-08 MUST write), the full audit matrix broken into three sub-tables (minimum / optional files / optional dirs), and out-of-canonical-shape flags (grime-time legacy `skills/`, gad + global legacy `config.json`, and three "thin" roots missing optional REQUIREMENTS/ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS). Aligned `gad projects audit` (the "gad health" surface) against the definition doc: new helper `computeCanonicalShape(planDir)` and four canonical-list constants (`CANONICAL_MINIMUM_FILES`, `CANONICAL_OPTIONAL_FILES`, `CANONICAL_LEGACY_FILES`, `CANONICAL_LEGACY_DIRS`) in `bin/gad.cjs`, each citing `references/project-shape.md` as source of truth. The audit command gains three additive per-root checks (`canonical_minimum`, `canonical_optional`, `canonical_legacy`) and a new "Canonical planning-root shape" summary block under the existing table; existing `required_files` / `recommended_files` / `sink_*` checks are untouched for backwards compat. Verification: `gad projects audit --project app-forge` reports `canonical_minimum 4/4 present` + `optional: REQUIREMENTS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`; same command for `grime-time` correctly flags `legacy: skills (project-local skill staging is deprecated — use .planning/proto-skills/ per decision gad-183)`. Decision gad-185 captures the full contract. **42.4-08 consumes `references/project-shape.md` as the contract for `gad projects init`** — §5 of that doc is the literal XML each scaffold file must produce, and §2 is the four-file minimum it must write on every invocation regardless of format flag. Out of scope + untouched: `gad projects init` (42.4-08 territory), `gad sink`/`gad docs` (42.4-10 territory), eval loader (42.4-15, already shipped).

task.goal: Audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdown→XML conversion gap before adding new commands. CORRECTION 2026-04-14: original framing of this task assumed no conversion path existed and proposed `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml`. User pointed out we already have `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/decompile/sync/diff/status/validate` — bidirectional sink flow exists. `gad sink decompile` specifically "ensures .planning/ dirs exist for all projects; creates stubs for missing source files" which is close to but not identical to the MD→XML conversion needed. Audit: does any existing command convert markdown PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/ROADMAP/STATE prose into canonical XML? If yes, document it and close this task. If no, decide whether to extend `gad sink decompile` to handle the case or add a focused subcommand. Don't add new commands until we've ruled out the existing surface.
task.resolution: Determination (C) — no existing command handles it. `sink compile` reads MD but passes it through into MDX without structuring; `sink decompile` creates empty XML shells (name is misleading, it does not read sink content); `migrate-schema` goes XML→MD, opposite direction. Verified by running all three against a temp dir with a markdown-only planning root; zero commands transferred MD prose into canonical XML. Full audit + spec for new `gad planning hydrate` subcommand (spec only, not implemented) in references/sink-md-xml-audit.md. Extending `sink decompile` was rejected because its documented invariant "never overwrites existing source files" conflicts with the useful MD→XML case. Follow-up task needed to implement hydrate (~150 LOC).

task.goal: Sink coherence investigation: map the full doc compilation pipeline so agents know which surface to edit when. Concern surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "I am pretty sure we have a planning sink set up. We need to investigate why at some point and make sure all our docs compile to the same place." The repo has `[planning] docs_sink = "docs"` in gad-config.toml, `[[docs.projects]]` and `[[planning.roots]]` registries, `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/sync/decompile/diff/status/validate`, plus `gad site compile` (phase 10). It's unclear from the outside which command is canonical for: (a) editing a planning root's ROADMAP/DECISIONS/etc, (b) updating a docs.projects entry, (c) keeping the compiled MDX in `docs/<id>/planning/` in sync with `.planning/` source, (d) what happens when both source and sink drift. Output: a short references/sink-pipeline.md doc explaining the full flow + a quick-reference table mapping "I want to edit X" → "use command Y" → "verify with Z." Then update relevant skills (new-project, plan-phase, execute-phase) to reference it.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Created `references/sink-pipeline.md` (the coherence map, one level above 42.4-10's per-command audit). Eight sections: purpose, source-of-truth statement ("XML under .planning/ is authoritative, everything else is derived"), the three registries (`[[planning.roots]]` / `[[docs.projects]]` / `[[evals.roots]]`) and their consumers, an ASCII flow diagram showing which arrows actually work vs which are aspirational (sink decompile + MD-mode sink compile flagged as broken), a 19-row quick-reference table mapping "I want to edit X → use command Y → verify with Z" covering hand-edit XML flows, sink compile/status/diff, docs.projects feature docs, site compile (phase 10), and explicit NOT SUPPORTED rows for MD→XML and MDX round-trip, a footguns list citing 42.4-10's audit plus two new ones (`site compile` and `sink compile` are independent axes that both read .planning/ directly; `docs_sink` is singular monorepo-wide), and related-docs links. Updated three skills (gad-new-project, gad-plan-phase, gad-execute-phase) with a short `<planning_state_surface>` block pointing at the new doc — no rewrites, one reference each. `gad-new-project` also gained a reference to `project-shape.md` in its execution_context. Doc is 124 lines (under the 300 line budget).

task.goal: Multi-root eval discovery. Add `[[evals.roots]]` array to `gad-config.toml` mirroring `[[planning.roots]]`. Default root stays `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/` for backwards-compat. New roots like parent-repo `evals/` (or `apps/forge/evals/`) become first-class. `gad eval list/run/preserve/verify/open/review/report` all walk the union of roots. Eval project ids must be unique across roots — duplicate detection at load time. Surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge planning: forge wants to consume evals from the parent custom_portfolio repo without polluting the framework submodule with downstream user projects, AND forge's own eval project (app-forge) needs to live outside the GAD submodule because forge is not a framework component. Blocks app-forge eval bootstrap (decision forge-09 plan/build separation requires fresh-agent spawn for any forge implementation work).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Added `[[evals.roots]]` TOML parsing + serialization to `bin/gad-config.cjs` (new `evalsRoots` field on the loaded config object, empty-array default for backwards compat across fromToml/fromJson/defaults paths). Introduced three helpers at the top of `bin/gad.cjs`: `getEvalRoots()` returns ordered absolute dirs of every root (walks upward from submodule findRepoRoot to locate the monorepo config that declares `[[evals.roots]]`, appends the submodule default implicitly, dedupes on absolute path), `listAllEvalProjects()` walks all roots and throws a load-time error on duplicate project ids naming both paths, `resolveEvalProject(name)` / `resolveOrDefaultEvalProjectDir(name)` map a project name to its owning root. Rewired every eval subcommand to use the helpers instead of hardcoded `path.join(gadDir, 'evals', …)`: list, run, score, diff, setup, status, runs, show, scores, suite, report, preserve, verify, review, open, readme, inherit-skills, trace (list/show/diff/report/write/init/log-cmd/log-skill/finalize/reconstruct/from-log), plus the `log --eval`, `worktree prune` preservation cross-check, `rounds --project`, and `listEvalProjectsHint` helper. Per-skill eval harness under `gad eval skill …` left untouched (different system, skills/<name>/evals/). Added `[[evals.roots]] id="app-forge" path="apps/forge/evals"` to repo-root `gad-config.toml` (also fixed adjacent `apps\forge` backslash typo in the `[[planning.roots]]` entry to `apps/forge`). Created `apps/forge/evals/.gitkeep`. Verification: `gad eval list` shows both existing evals from the submodule, `gad eval verify` passes clean, temporarily dropping `apps/forge/evals/escape-the-dungeon/project.json` triggers the duplicate-id error naming both absolute paths. No new decision file entry — the "implicit default root + additive configured roots + load-time duplicate detection" rule is captured in the helper doc comments in `bin/gad.cjs` and can be promoted to DECISIONS.xml later if we add a second policy knob.

task.goal: New skill + slash command `/gad-eval-spawn` for kicking off eval runs from inside a Claude Code session. Wraps `gad eval run --project <name> --execute` to get the JSON spec, then uses the orchestrator's Agent/spawn capability to launch a worktree-isolated Claude (or other runtime) agent against the spec. Writes a session marker (probably under `.planning/.eval-runs/<run-id>.json`) so the orchestrator knows which run is in flight, can poll status, and can preserve outputs via `gad eval preserve` after completion. Authored via the `create-skill` skill so we dogfood the skill-authoring pattern. Skill name: `gad-eval-spawn`. Slash command: `/gad-eval-spawn <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`. Surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "we need a skill use create skill and also our command workflow pattern for /gad-project-eval-run or whatever we should call it to actually kick start the flow in claude code." Per decision forge-09 (plan/build separation), this skill is the seam between the orchestrating session and the spawned builder session.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Created canonical skill `skills/gad-eval-spawn/` with `SKILL.md`, `COMMAND.md`, and `skill.json` following the existing `gad-eval-run` / `gad-eval-bootstrap` pattern (canonical frontmatter `name: gad:eval-spawn`, folded `>-` description, argument-hint `<project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`, allowed-tools: Read/Write/Bash/Agent). Procedure documents the full plan/build seam: resolve project via `gad eval list`, call `gad eval run --project <name> --runtime <r> --baseline <b> --execute` to produce EXEC.json (the CLI already emits this between `--- EXEC_JSON_START/END ---` markers — no CLI change needed), refuse if `.planning/.eval-runs/<project>-<version>.json` marker already exists, create worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-<project>-<version>` from `--baseline`, write session marker with `status: "running"` + runId/project/version/species/generation/worktreePath/runDir/agentId/startedAt, spawn `general-purpose` subagent with `isolation: "worktree"`, cwd + prompt + env pulled verbatim from EXEC.json (never rewrap the prompt — it's already fully hydrated), on completion update marker → `agent-complete`, run `gad eval preserve <project> <version> --from <worktree>` (mandatory per gad-38) → `preserved`, then `gad worktree clean` → `cleaned`. Failure modes: spawn-fail removes marker, agent-fail leaves worktree for forensics, preserve-fail blocks cleanup, duplicate marker refuses. `.planning/.eval-runs/` added to submodule .gitignore as transient runtime state. `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` passes. Cited decisions forge-09 (plan/build seam) and gad-38 (preservation contract). Dogfood note: the `create-skill` skill's YAML frontmatter rules (folded `>-`, colons-safe, indentation) guided the SKILL.md shape cleanly — no friction. `gad eval run --execute` already existed at bin/gad.cjs:1932+ and emits the EXEC.json shape used by this skill (runId is derived as `<project>-<version>` since the CLI keys on version rather than a separate run-id field), so no CLI addition was needed.

task.goal: Audit and finish phase 43 species.json migration. Phase 43 unified the eval schema around `evals/<project>/project.json` + `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json`, but escape-the-dungeon's existing species directories still use `gad.json` instead of `species.json`. Decide canonical name (recommend: `species.json` per phase 43 spec) and either: (a) rename `gad.json` → `species.json` across all existing species in `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/`, OR (b) update phase 43 prose + the CLI loader to treat `gad.json` as the canonical name and abandon `species.json`. User direction 2026-04-14: do the rename to species.json. Update CLI loaders, build-site-data.mjs, eval-preservation tests, and any references in skills/workflows/docs. Pick one shape so app-forge's species file isn't ahead of reality and so the project ⊇ species inheritance contract (42.4-15) has a stable file shape to read from.

Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Shipped: new `lib/eval-loader.cjs` exporting `loadProject`, `loadSpecies`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics per decision gad-184: project defaults → inherits_from chain (deepest ancestor first) → target species; last write wins; arrays REPLACE not merge; species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) do not back-propagate to project; cycles throw. Wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` to the loader and swapped `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` to use `loadResolvedSpecies` so the site sees the merged shape. Hermetic tests in `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` cover (a)-(e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + pure-function spot check (7/7 pass via `node --test`). Other legacy `gad.json` reads in bin/gad.cjs (eval run, eval verify, eval review, eval open/report) are orthogonal rename fallout and left for a separate surgical pass.

Forge proving-ground spec: formalize "forge produces a passing escape-the-dungeon generation from its existing REQUIREMENTS.md via the editor's spawn flow" as a measurable acceptance criterion. Decision forge-11 captures the why. Output: checklist doc at `apps/forge/.planning/plans/forge-proving-ground.md` defining PG-01..PG-09 (build preserved, clean headless load, zero console.error, game root mounts, player renders, tilemap renders, input moves player, no unhandled rejections, probe runtime < 30s). Historical comparison table retroactively scores all 12 ETD gad-species generations (v1..v12): 4 have preserved builds (v7, v8, v9, v10), exactly 1 (v8) has an explicit SCORE.md "Build requirement gate PASSED" annotation, and PG-02..PG-08 are `?` across the board because no mechanical probe has ever run against an ETD build. GAPs flagged: no Playwright harness in repo (phase 08 scope), no canonical ETD root selector / player hook, input scheme varies arrow vs WASD. DOG-02 in `apps/forge/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml` and phase 08 success criteria in `apps/forge/.planning/ROADMAP.xml` both updated to cite the checklist. Last of the 42.4-12..16 framework gates blocking app-forge phase 01.

Implement `gad planning hydrate` — the MD → XML inverse of `gad docs compile`. Closes the follow-up flagged by task 42.4-10's audit (references/sink-md-xml-audit.md §6). Subcommand walks configured planning roots (or `--projectid`/`--all`), reads sibling markdown for each canonical slot (STATE, ROADMAP, DECISIONS, TASK-REGISTRY, REQUIREMENTS), and writes canonical XML into the root's `.planning/` directory. Supports `--from <dir>` to read MD from an external source dir (default is the planning dir itself), `--dry-run` to print the generated XML without writing, and `--force` to overwrite existing XML (prior XML is archived under `.planning/archive/xml/<ts>/` first). Non-force runs skip slots where the XML already exists, preserving the "XML is authoritative" invariant from decision 42.4-10.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Added five inverse parsers (`stateMdToXml`, `roadmapMdToXml`, `decisionsMdToXml`, `taskRegistryMdToXml`, `requirementsMdToXml`) plus a `hydrateFromMd(planDir, fromDir, options)` orchestrator to `lib/docs-compiler.cjs` — each parser mirrors its `*XmlToMd` sibling field-for-field and reuses the same slot shape. Hydrator walks a new `HYDRATE_MAP` (parallel to `SOURCE_MAP`), honours `force`/`dryRun`, and returns `{ written, skipped, archived, results }` for CLI rendering. Added `planning` top-level subcommand group to `bin/gad.cjs` with a single `hydrate` subcommand (args: `--projectid`, `--all`, `--from`, `--dry-run`, `--force`); registered in main subCommands map between `docs` and `site`. Smoke-tested all five parsers against hand-crafted MD input (every canonical field round-trips), end-to-end hydrator run through a temp dir verified write + skip-when-exists + force-with-archive behavior, and CLI `--help` surface confirmed. LOC: lib/docs-compiler.cjs +267, bin/gad.cjs +59 (~326 total, close to the audit's ~150 LOC estimate — overshoot is from the inverse parsers being heavier than the forward ones because of paragraph/heading splitting). Closes 42.4-10's follow-up; the audit doc's open design questions (bidirectional-safety, `projects init` integration) are deliberately left as separate follow-ups.

task.goal: Delete dead project-level `gad.json` fallthrough reads in `bin/gad.cjs` (decision gad-184). Task 42.4-14 renamed `evals/<project>/gad.json` to species-level `evals/<project>/species/<sp>/species.json`, but five call sites in `bin/gad.cjs` (buildEvalPrompt / eval run metadata + traceScaffold / eval verify / eval review --rubric / eval readme) still read from the old project-level path. Because the file no longer exists, every read silently fell back to `{}` and every downstream field rendered as a default or dash — legacy fallback fallout flagged in task 42.4-15's note. Delete the reads; inline defaults where a field must remain; rewire `eval review --rubric` and `eval readme` through `lib/eval-loader.cjs::loadAllResolvedSpecies` so they pull `humanReviewRubric` / description / domain / tech stack / build requirement from the resolved-species shape instead.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Five sites edited in `bin/gad.cjs`: (1) `buildEvalPrompt` — removed the `gad.json` cfg load and the brownfield-baseline block that depended on it (block was already unreachable). (2) `eval run` traceScaffold — removed the `gadJson` variable and inlined `eval_type: 'greenfield'`, `workflow: 'unknown'`, `domain/tech_stack/build_requirement: null`. (3) `eval verify` audit — dropped the `cfg.type`/`cfg.constraints.planning_only` branch and hardcoded `evalType = 'implementation'` (the only value the block ever produced post-rename). (4) `eval review --rubric` — rewired to call `loadAllResolvedSpecies(projectDir)` and walk resolved species looking for `humanReviewRubric` (camelCase canonical per 42.4-15) with a legacy `human_review_rubric` fallback so the command keeps working before task 42.4-19 converts the species.json files. (5) `eval readme` — rewired the project-metadata header table to use `loadEvalProject` + first-resolved-species for description/domain/mode/workflow/tech-stack/build-requirement. Every remaining `gad.json` literal in the file is either the canonical legacy-files list entry (line 985) or a "this was removed in 42.4-18" comment. Syntax check + `gad eval list` smoke test pass. Coordinated with parallel 42.4-17 planning-hydrate work by staying clear of `planningCmd` / `planningHydrateCmd` (lines ~2005-2060).

task.goal: Normalize species.json keys to camelCase (decision gad-184, follow-up to task 42.4-15). The project ⊇ species inheritance loader (`lib/eval-loader.cjs`) does literal key matching via `applyLayer`, so when `project.json` declares `techStack` (camelCase) and a species declares `tech_stack` (snake_case), the species override silently fails and both keys end up on the merged shape. Per gad-184 contract, camelCase is canonical. Rename `tech_stack`, `build_requirement`, `human_review_rubric`, and `context_framework` to camelCase across every species.json under `evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/*/` and `evals/gad-explainer-video/species/*/`; confirm the eval loader needs no special-casing (it does not — literal-match is the point); update all consumers reading those snake_case keys.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. (1) All 6 species.json files updated — tech_stack → techStack, build_requirement → buildRequirement, human_review_rubric → humanReviewRubric, context_framework → contextFramework. Species-only `inherits_from` left alone because it is the loader's documented API contract. Nested rubric dimension keys (ui_polish, mechanics_implementation, etc.) left alone because they are rubric data values, not inheritance-level object keys. `constraints.uses_gad_framework` etc. similarly left alone — constraints are replaced wholesale, no key-clash issue. (2) Consumer updates: `bin/gad.cjs eval list` (dropped `|| projectCfg.tech_stack` fallbacks on the list loop and the first-species fallback), `bin/gad.cjs eval review --rubric` (dropped the legacy `human_review_rubric` fallback I wired temporarily in 42.4-18), `bin/gad.cjs eval readme` (dropped the `tech_stack` / `build_requirement` fallbacks in the pick() chain), `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalSpecies` (reads `data.humanReviewRubric`, `data.buildRequirement`, `data.contextFramework` directly; dropped snake_case fallbacks on lines 512/529/531/532), `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalParents` (dropped `|| p.meta.tech_stack`), `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs inferWorkflow` (reads `data.contextFramework` instead of `data.context_framework`). (3) `lib/score-generator.cjs` also carried a dead `gad.json` read for `build_requirement`; rewired through `loadAllResolvedSpecies` + `buildRequirement` with a `trace.build_requirement` fallback (TRACE.json stays snake_case — that's a schema field on already-written artifacts, out of scope). (4) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` JSDoc updated (`human_review_rubric` → `humanReviewRubric`). Verification: hermetic inheritance check via `loadAllResolvedSpecies` confirms the `techStack: "kaplay"` (escape-the-dungeon) and `techStack: "remotion"` (gad-explainer-video) project defaults now correctly propagate through to every species, and `humanReviewRubric` resolves with the expected dimension counts (bare 5, emergent 6, gad 5 for ETD; gad 5 for explainer video). `gad eval list` smoke test passes.

task.goal: Delete the conditions being dropped from the eval matrix: escape-the-dungeon-gad-emergent (D-20), escape-the-dungeon-planning-only (D-21), all etd-brownfield-* (D-22, drops greenfield/brownfield mode framing entirely), and all etd-{babylonjs,phaser,pixijs,threejs} tech-stack variants (D-23, not testing tech-stack variation under the new schema). Also delete corresponding preserved builds under apps/portfolio/public/evals/ if present. Single commit.

Physical merge of escape-the-dungeon family (gad/bare/emergent) into one parent dir with species/ subdirs (D-09, D-26). Same merge for gad-explainer-video family (D-25). Use git mv via temp-rename intermediate so directory history is preserved. After this lands, evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/{gad,bare,emergent}/ contains the old sibling generations.

Define the new gad.json schema (project.json + species.json split) and migrate every project to it. Drop eval_mode field everywhere (D-22). Stub cardImage/tagline/heroImage fields for phase 44 to populate (D-14). Drop compare_to references to deleted projects. Per follow-up: single-variant projects (cli-efficiency, reader-workspace, etc.) deleted entirely — only escape-the-dungeon and gad-explainer-video remain.

Migrate preserved build artifact paths under apps/portfolio/public/evals/ from flat-name to project/species nesting. escape-the-dungeon-bare/v3/ becomes escape-the-dungeon/bare/v3/ etc. Use git mv. Honors decision gad-38 by keeping every preserved build readable, just at a new addressable path (D-27).

Rewrite scanEvalProjects() and scanProducedArtifacts() in site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs to walk the new evals/<project>/species/<species>/v<N>/ layout. Replace the hardcoded escape-the-dungeon project list. Update the keys (currently project/version) to project/species/version. Updated 8 walker sites + 2 helpers (listEvalSpecies, listEvalGenerations, listEvalProjects). Site/public/playable migrated to same layout.

Update the TypeScript types in site/lib/eval-data.generated.ts (and the source-of-truth interface) to add species, dna, cardImage, tagline, heroImage fields and remove evalMode. The generated TS file is regenerated by build-site-data.mjs and contains the new fields automatically.

Update tests/eval-preservation.test.cjs (and any other tests broken by the schema rename) to expect the new species/generation paths. The test now asserts both positive (every (project, species) pair has TRACE.json + run/ + preserved build at the new paths) and negative (no flat-layout dirs remain at the top level of evals/ or apps/portfolio/public/evals/). All 8 tests passing. Two pre-existing-data-loss generations exempted via KNOWN_PRE_PHASE_43_DATA_LOSS set with documentation.

Update site display strings across vendor/get-anything-done/site/. Replaced user-facing "Round N" → "Evolution N" and "Round by round" → "Evolution by evolution" across 18 site files (hypotheses-data, roadmap-shared, MethodologyCompositeWeightsCatalog, content-driven, findings, projects, freedom, requirements, methodology template matrix). Internal parsers that read "Round " literals updated in lockstep. Dropped greenfield/brownfield framing entirely — incidental copy stripped, ProjectsIntro rewritten around species, DOMAIN_MAP collapsed to two surviving projects, projects-shared collapsed from 6 mode-keyed entries to 3 species-keyed entries. Deleted EvalLineageGraph + MethodologyLineageSection (their only purpose was the dropped lineage framing). Added species/id fields to EvalRunRecord/EvalTemplateAsset/PlanningZipAsset/EvalProjectMeta/CurrentRequirementsFile interfaces. Build runs clean, all 8 preservation tests pass, type errors limited to 5 pre-existing issues unrelated to phase 43.

Update gad CLI help text, command surfaces, and snapshot output to use the new vocabulary. gad eval list shows species/generation rows. gad eval preserve gets a new signature that takes project + species + generation. Old --version flag either errors with a redirect or accepts the new shape.

Update AGENTS.md, the Loop docs, README, and CHANGELOG with the new vocabulary. Add the single transitional note to PROJECT.md (or top of ROADMAP.xml) per D-16 explaining that pre-43 phases used "round" for what we now call "evolution" and "vN" per project for what we now call "generation" per species.

Update ROADMAP.xml phase 44.5 title from "Brood Editor" to "Species Editor" and rewrite its goal to drop "brood" / "Brood Editor" references, reserve /project-editor route name, and embed the Lovable/Bolt-style species-without-build visualization scope (D-17, D-18). Update phase 45 goal to drop the "v4 brood" example phrasing. Closed phase prose stays verbatim per D-15.

/project-market becomes a real multi-project launcher inside the vendor site. Today /project-market is a single static page; it must list every registered GAD planning root (read from `gad-config.toml` [[planning.roots]] + [[evals.roots]]), render one card per project with species count, latest generation per species, and a "launch eval" affordance. The launch affordance routes to a dev-only handler that invokes `gad eval run --project <id> --execute` via the phase-44.5 command bridge (or a stub that prints the command when the bridge isn't present yet). Any GAD-based project that vendors this site inherits the same surface automatically — the site is "about the project it is launched from" and its children. First cut: static card grid driven by build-site-data reading the workspace roots; launch button is wired to a placeholder route until 44.5-02 lands. Captures the user's constraint that site-visible management is a hard requirement for any non-eval loop work.

Scaffold the brood editor route: /brood-editor (or /projects/[id]/edit) gated to NODE_ENV=development, renders a project's eval folder as an editable tree. Reuse the phase 44 marketplace card data but flip affordances from browse to mutate. First cut is read-only view of the tree + "this would be editable in dev mode" placeholder so we can ship the shell before the mutation bridge.

Dev-server command bridge: Node child-process bridge behind a dev-only API route that rejects all requests unless NODE_ENV=development. First cut is permissive: accepts any `gad *` and any `npx *` command so we can iterate on the editor surface without fighting the allow-list. Hardening (explicit allow-list, argument sanitization, per-command timeout tuning) is deferred to a follow-up task after the editor's happy path is proven. Stream stdout/stderr back to the browser via Server-Sent Events or similar. Explicitly rejects production requests at module load time so the wide-open dev surface can never ship in prod.

Hardening pass for the dev-server command bridge: after 44.5-02 proves the editor works, replace the permissive "any gad/npx" acceptance with an explicit allow-list (`gad evolution status|validate|promote|discard`, `gad snapshot`, `gad state`, `gad tasks`, `npx` commands scoped to the eval repo, `pnpm|npm|yarn` install/build/test), add argument sanitization, per-command timeout tuning, and structured error responses. Keep the NODE_ENV=development gate as the primary safety — the allow-list is defense in depth.

Live-edit eval folder from the editor: file-system adapter that reads/writes requirements.md, gad.json, per-species overrides, and manifest entries for the currently-selected project, guarded by the same dev-only gate as 44.5-02. No schema validation in this task — focus on the round-trip (read file → edit in browser → write file → trigger rebuild).

Species/population/generation/brood capture surfaces inside the editor: small inspector panes showing the currently-edited project's species rows, each species's generations, and the brood view across all species at the same round. Reuses phase 44 components but with an "active editing" affordance indicating which row is being mutated.
